channel.title: Courses on Pieter Belmans
channel.description: Recent content in Courses on Pieter Belmans

item.description:
Exceptional collections for finite-dimensional algebras and partial flag varieties

In the Sommersemester 2020 we will run a seminar on exceptional collections in representation theory, first discussing the general theory for finite-dimensional algebras, and then we focus on derived categories of partial flag varieties and the representation theory of algebraic groups. The goal is to

  • get familiar with exceptional collections and their applications in algebra, algebraic geometry and representation theory
  • discuss them in the context of coherent sheaves, and study the conjecture that the derived category of a partial flag variety has a full exceptional collection

We have a detailed program.

This is the schedule for the working group seminar for the representation theory group at the University of Bonn. This semester's topic is Heisenberg categorification (maybe we will cover double affine Hecke algebras later in the semester).

The seminar runs Friday afternoon, 14:15–15:45, in 1.008.

April 12
Catharina Stroppel
Heisenberg algebras and a graphical calculus
April 25 (!)
Hankyung Ko
The quantum Heisenberg algebra
This talk is at 16:15–17:45, in the Zeichensaal, Wegelerstraße 10
May 3
Vinoth Nandakumar
The quantum Heisenberg algebra (II)
May 10
Tomasz Przezdziecki
The elliptic Hall algebra
cancelled
May 16 (!)
Catharina Stroppel
The elliptic Hall algebra
May 17
Thorsten Heidersdorf
Hochschild homology of the quantum Heisenberg category
May 24
Ruslan Maksimau
Properties of elliptic Hall algebras
May 31
Pieter Belmans
The 2-category $\operatorname{Hilb}S$
June 7
Rostislav Devyatov
The Heisenberg action on $\operatorname{Hilb}S$
June 21
Emily Norton
Action of the quantum Heisenberg category on module categories for Hecke and Cherednik algebras

Selected topics in representation theory: Hochschild (co)homology

Description

The goal of the course is to give an introduction to Hochschild (co)homology, focussing on its applications in deformation theory of algebras (and schemes), and the role of the Hochschild–Kostant–Rosenberg decomposition in all this. There will be three parts:

  1. Hochschild (co)homology for algebras and deformation theory
  2. Hochschild (co)homology and derived categories
  3. The Hochschild–Kostant–Rosenberg decomposition for smooth varieties

The lecture notes of a previous installment of this course are available, and give you an idea of what will be covered (although there will be differences).

Advanced topics in algebra: Hochschild (co)homology

Description

The goal of the course is to give an introduction to Hochschild (co)homology, focussing on its applications in deformation theory of algebras (and schemes), and the role of the Hochschild–Kostant–Rosenberg decomposition in all this. There will be three parts:

  1. Hochschild (co)homology for algebras
  2. Hochschild (co)homology and derived categories
  3. The Hochschild–Kostant–Rosenberg decomposition for schemes

The lecture notes are available. All comments are welcome!

There will be an oral exam.

Problem list for the homological projective duality seminar

On this page we can collect some interesting questions, projects and problems for the homological projective duality seminar. They are in no particular order. They might be trivial, or impossible, or wrong, or somehow all three of them simultaneously.

If you want to add something, please get in touch with either Daniel or me. If you want to work on something, please get in touch with either Daniel, me, or the person who came up with the description.

Quantum cohomology of partial flag varieties

In the Wintersemester 2020–2021 we will run a seminar on quantum cohomology of partial flag varieties, introducing a second theme to the symphony which is the geometry of partial flag varieties (the first theme being the structure of the derived category, as discussed in the previous seminar). The goal is to:

  • introduce quantum cohomology, as a deformation of the usual cohomology of a variety using Gromov–Witten invariants;
  • describe methods to work with the quantum cohomology of partial flag varieties;
  • give structural results of the quantum cohomology of partial flag varieties, and understand their link to the structure of the derived category;

with a view towards current research. For the majority of the time the previous seminar is not a prerequisite.

Here is the distribution of the groups for Representation theory I, in the summer term of 2019. Please let me know if I have misspellt your name.

Monday

  • Peerenboom
  • Wojciechowski
  • Klemenc
  • Schremmer
  • Spellmann
  • Grochowicz
  • Maibach
  • Moektijono
  • Isem
  • Wu
  • Wang
  • Cheng
  • Seifner
  • Hoffmann
  • Garcia Magia
  • Ronchi
  • Amala Santos
  • Zhang
  • Cordova
  • Nguyen
  • Linskens

Wednesday

  • Vanni
  • Koinemann
  • Nehme
  • Linshens
  • Kruck
  • Busse
  • Hitschke
  • Dimitris
  • Beuce Saindon
  • Schirren
  • Kretschmer
  • Dong
  • Fellhauer

Thursday

  • Wehihan
  • Roggendoff
  • Zon
  • Lachmann
  • Blödtner
  • Puhlmann
  • Siebler
  • Akynboer
  • Wild
  • Bonfert
  • Niemann
  • Hostzsig
  • Friederichs
  • Palsga
  • Mizyuk
  • Hu
  • Lorke
  • How
  • Cristian
  • Nöller
  • Yang
  • De Vries
  • Hippold

Representation theory II

This page contains the information about the course V4A4 Representation Theory II, taught by Prof. Dr. Catharina Stroppel.

This course will give an introduction into some aspects of geometric representation theory. We will start by studying flag varieties and Grassmannians. Important hereby are in particular the Schubert cells which are the cells in some affine cellular decomposition of these varieties. Moreover we will spend some time on describing these varieties as varieties with a torus action. Then the connection to cohomology theories will be made.

Representation theory I

This page contains the information about the course V4A3 Representation Theory I, taught by Prof. Dr. Catharina Stroppel.

In this course we will study complex semisimple Lie algebras, their classification and their representation theory.

Lie algebras arise naturally as tangent spaces of Lie groups or affine algebraic groups. Their representation theory is a model for many other — often more complicated and more involved — structures in representation theory. We in particular will learn the concept of (highest) weight theory and the basic concepts of reflection groups. The first part will follow standard literature like [H], [K]. The second part will be on root systems, integrality questions and Chevalley groups. If time allows we will finally deal with the famous category O of infinite dimensional highest weight modules.

Seminar in Algebraic Geometry

The topic is noncommutative algebraic geometry. First we will discuss some noncommutative projective geometry. Then we will discuss some finite-over-the-center geometry.

The seminar runs Thursday mornings, 11:00–12:45. The location is BBG - 005. In January, we will be in BBG - 077.

The schedule (still subject to changes in the latter half) is the following.

September 11
Pieter Belmans
An introduction to noncommutative projective geometry
September 18
no seminar (CAGE in Utrecht)
September 25
Anton Odina
Constructing varieties using Spec and Proj
October 2
Deik van der Horst
Coherent sheaves on varieties
October 9
Nico Puthu Parackat Biosca
Abelian categories
October 16
Valentijn van der Land
Sheaf cohomology and projective space
October 23
Matthijs Holstege
Artin–Schelter regular algebras
October 30
Anton Odina
Quotients of abelian categories
November 6
no seminar (From 0 to $p$ and back in Utrecht)
November 13
Deik van der Horst
Point modules
November 20
Matthijs Holstege
Classification of Artin–Schelter regular algebras in dimensions 2 and 3
November 27
Okke van Garderen
Central Proj construction
December 4
Pieter Belmans
Point modules for higher-dimensional Artin–Schelter regular algebras
December 11
Pieter Belmans
Noncommutative plane curves
December 18
no seminar (FanoHK closing conference)
January 8
Pieter Belmans
Graded Clifford algebras
January 15
Pieter Belmans
Noncommutative projective varieties
January 22
no seminar
January 29

Thesis day April 2019

On thesis day (which is in fact 2 days) bachelor, master and PhD students, and postdocs, associated to the group of Prof. Catharina Stroppel give short talks about their progress and results.

Friday April 26

14:00–14:40
Thorsten Heidersdorf
How zero is zero?
14:45–15:10
Tim Blödtner
Galois extensions of commutative rings
15:15–15:40
Hanna Busse
Quasi-erbliche Algebren
15:45–16:15
break
16:15–16:55
Felix Schremmer
Hochschild cohomology of weighted projective lines
17:00–17:25
Finn Flug
The skeleton category of $\underline{\operatorname{Rep}}\mathrm{GL}{}_\delta$
17:30–17:55
Luise Puhlmann
Flag varieties and Springer fibers

Saturday April 27

9:30–10:10
Tashi Walde
Reflection functors for derived quiver representations
10:15–10:40
Jonas Nehme
Robinson–Schensted Korrespondenz
10:45–11:10
break
11:15–11:55
Anna Mkrtchyan
Gradings on the Brauer algebra
12:00–12:25
Alexandra Niemann
Tilted algebras
12:30–...
lunch

Thesis day July 2019

On thesis day bachelor, master and PhD students, and postdocs, associated to the group of Prof. Catharina Stroppel give short talks about their progress and results.

Monday July 29

09:15–09:45
Joanna Meinel
Gradings and Hochschild cohomology
10:00–10:30
Hanna Busse
epsilon-stratifizierte Algebren
10:45–11:15
Finn Flug
Deligne's category and an application to representations of $\mathfrak{gl}(m|n)$
11:30–12:00
Ulukbek Akynbaev
tbd

Thesis day June 2019

On thesis day (which is in fact 2 days) bachelor, master and PhD students, and postdocs, associated to the group of Prof. Catharina Stroppel give short talks about their progress and results.

Friday June 14

16:15–16:40
Hankyung Yo
Why taking quotients geometrically is nontrivial
16:50–17:15
David Meyer
Differential equations and modules over the Weyl algebra
17:15–17:35
break
17:35–18:00
Florine Hartwig
GIT applied to quiver representations
18:05–18:30
Till Wehrhan
Enumerative geometry in $\mathbb{P}^3$

Saturday June 15

9:30–9:55
Daniel Tubbenhauer
Fun with semigroups
10:00–10:25
Zbigniew Wojciechowski
Primitive idempotents in $\mathbb{C}\mathrm{S}_d$
10:30–10:55
David Kruck
The Harish-Chandra isomorphism
10:55–11:20
break
11:20–11:45
Joanna Meinel
Gradings & Hochschild cohomology
11:50–12:15
Malte Kornemann
Sheaves on graphs
12:20–12:45
Robin Louis
The Tannakian formalism

Thesis day June 2021

On thesis day (which is in fact 2 days) bachelor, master and PhD students associated to the group of Prof. Catharina Stroppel give short talks about their progress and results.

Friday June 4

14:00–14:30
Till Wehrhan
Yang-Baxter algebra operation on the cohomology of Grassmannians
14:40:–15:10
Liao Wang
On combinatorial models for cohomology rings
15:20–15:50
Amine Koubaa
Comparing Brauer groups
16:00–16:30
Thilo Baumann
Quadrics and Koszul algebras

Saturday June 5

10:00–10:20
Ellis Hedwig
The Picard group of a ring
10:25–10:55
Zbigniew Wojiechowski
Generalisations of Jones-Wenzl projectors
11:00–11:30
Tim Bloedtner
Towards a diagramatics of singular Soergel bimodules
11:45–12:15
Daniel Bermudez
$\mathrm{E}_n$-algebras for representation theorists
12:20–12:50
Lukas Bonfert
Translation functors for $q(2)$ with a glimpse for the general case
